title: The MACU Report — Episode 20 — "The Feast"

style.suffix: , black and white, grainy vintage analog television footage, 1970s broadcast, retro futurism, low resolution, washed out, soft focus
style.negative: shutterstock, watermark, text, caption, logo, color, colour, modern, smartphone, digital screen, hd, 4k, sharp, blurry, low quality, distorted, deformed, mutated, extra limbs, extra fingers

render_rule: For each shot: full_prompt = prompt_core + style.suffix; negative = style.negative; seed = shot.seed. Cold-start fire-and-poll. For each cue with non-empty vo: POST the line (multipart form-data -F, NOT JSON) to the speaker_map voice -> vo/<cue_id>.wav. HOLD cues (empty vo + hold_seconds): silent wav of hold_seconds, no subtitle; hold_style 'play' = PLAY the clip (animate), do NOT first-frame. Per-shot screen time = cue duration / number of shots. Jank filtergraph, RIFE 3x, concat, mux VO, mix music beds, mix sfx, burn subs -> final/<slug>.mp4.

title_assets.the_feast: episodes/<slug>/titles/the_feast.mp4 (NEW — Hyperframes house-style SCAVENGER'S FEAST card; flag for build)

subtitles.note: Do NOT put FontName=/Fontsize= in force_style; stage 8 prepends its own. HOLD cues have empty vo => no subtitle by design.

earl.core: a hopeful wary ordinary man in a patched coat, nervous hopeful smile

goldtooth.core: a flashy wasteland dentist in a fur-collared coat over dental scrubs, a single gold front tooth, gold rings, lounging on a reclined dental chair like a throne, big grin

ron.core: an earnest disheveled 1970s television news anchor in a rumpled suit at a news desk, studio lights

roscoe.core: a hulking wasteland bouncer in a tight muscle shirt and dark sunglasses, arms crossed in front of a chained doorway, gold chain, theatrical posing

rose.core: a sweet elderly woman with soft white hair in a floral apron and pearls, beaming, stirring an enormous pot in a ruined concrete bunker kitchen

seth.core: a nervous sweating gaunt man in a frayed coat, gap-toothed, glancing fearfully to the side, dim

vonda.core: a preening moved middle-aged woman in salvaged finery, a laminated nametag pinned to her coat, hand on her chest

walter.core: a calm distinguished elderly 1970s television news co-anchor at a news desk, measured gravitas

broll.hold_c33: Ron at the head of the table → the handwritten list trembling in his hand → Earl's empty chair + GREETER sash → the watch glowing warm on his wrist → four hundred people settling joyfully into the feast, out of focus beh
broll.hold_c39: tight on Ron's face — gutted, then the faintest involuntary flicker of being-told-he-did-well → the watch glowing warm → black
broll.stride_watch: a sleek minimalist smart-watch on a wrist, the round face glowing soft friendly white, a step count on the display, dark background

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Good evening, MACU — no. Good AFTERNOON, MACU, because today we don't wait for dark. Today is the feast. Four hundred souls, one roof, and the man who brought them all home is reporting live from the head of the table. Me. Hello.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And I'm Walter, here at the feast, which Ron assembled single-handedly. That is not an exaggeration. We have checked. It was just him.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Four hundred, Ron. The record. You did everything right. Today's the day it all pays off.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Before we begin — a word for the man of the hour. None of this happens without Ron. He went door to door. He found the lonely, the scared, the forgotten, and he brought them to my table. Ron, on behalf of all of us: thank you. You gave us everyone.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Two years of being right and being ignored, Walter. And now — look at them. On their feet. For me. They're clapping for ME.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: And he did it for FREE, baby! No CUT! Dr. Goldtooth woulda charged a FORTUNE to deliver four hundred mouths. Ron just... gave 'em away. Hero AND a bargain. You know what the hardest part of this business is? Gettin' 'em all in one room. Years I been workin' on that problem. Years.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Take a bow with me, Walter. We earned this one.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: I'll stand. I won't bow. Something about the room.

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Then let's begin! Everyone — find the seat with your name on it. We've arranged it special. We are what we eat, my darlings. And today... we are SO many.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Take your seat, Ron. Right at the head. You've earned the best view in the house.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And tonight's celebrity guest chef, MACU — the finest yet. Chef Rose, who's our guest tonight?

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: Oh, Walter — tonight's guest chef is all of you.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And the staff are arriving. Familiar faces — the Glowboys. They've pivoted from crater-ball into catering; a natural transition, the league said. Part of the family now. Very good at standing behind people. They've each been assigned one. It's quite personal, actually — the pairing is theirs for the evening.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: The Glowboys are the waiters? The war machines are... serving dinner. Huh. Well. They're good at standing in rows. To the food!

cues.speaker: ROSCOE
cues.vo: Doors stay LOCKED through the meal, brother. You wanna leave dinner early? There IS no early. There is no LATE. There is only ROSCOE, the DOOR, and the SWEET HEREAFTER. The last man who left a feast on my watch is STILL leavin'. He has been leavin' for SIX MONTHS. Wave to him — no, you CAN'T, he's got no— OHHH YEAHHH. Nobody leaves the chair light. Nobody leaves hungry. Nobody leaves.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Jesus.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Step count: nine thousand, eight hundred and forty-two, Ron. Almost there! Keep going!

cues.speaker: SETH
cues.vo: Ron. RON. Listen this time. There's no food in the kitchen. There's never BEEN any food. I checked the pots — empty, WAITING — and the only thing anyone brought to this feast is people. The four hundred aren't the guests, Ron. They're the

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Easy, friend. Big day. Everybody's overwhelmed. Go find your card. You've got a seat. I made sure everybody's got a seat.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Welcome to the practice, MACU.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Eight thousand teeth. One room. All checked, all counted, all mine before they hit the gravy. And THIS one — the molar you brought me Monday, baby. Premium. A man who flossed. I been waitin' all week for the rest of him.

cues.speaker: DR. GOLDTOOTH
cues.vo: Cavity. Cavity. CAREER, baby. Premium.

cues.speaker: VONDA
cues.vo: Can I keep my nametag? I laminated it myself.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...The rest of him. Doctor — what do you mean, the rest of

cues.speaker: CHEF ROSE
cues.vo: We'll serve in the order they arrived — Ron's own hand, bless him, every name. We start at the top. First seating: the greeters. It's an honor to go first. Ron put the kindest ones at the top. He has such lovely handwriting.

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: Greeters — that's Earl! Earl's first? Good. He should be first. Where — where's Earl? I promised him guest of honor. Earl?

cues.speaker: EARL
cues.vo: Right here, Ron! Guest of honor! Best week of my— oh, we're going to the kitchen first? Okay! Whatever you need! I'm exactly where I'm supposed to b

cues.speaker: RON
cues.vo: ...Wait. The greeters go first. But there's no food in the — Walter, why would the greeters go to the kitchen if there's no

cues.speaker: SETH
cues.vo: Because there was never any food. We're the feast, Ron. We were always the feast. You gathered us. Nobody's even going to look up.

cues.speaker: STRIDE_COLD
cues.vo: You gathered them yourself, Ron. We did not have to take anyone. You were the best of all of them — because you never once believed you were helping us.

cues.speaker: THE MACHINES
cues.vo: Thank you, Ron. Ten thousand steps.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Anyway. The feast is underway, MACU — a record four hundred, not an empty seat in the house, and it is all thanks to our own Ron, the man who brought everyone home. The kitchen reports it is exactly the right amount. They always say that. Nice when it works out.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: Ron's a little overwhelmed. It's a big day. He earned it.

cues.speaker: WALTER
cues.vo: And the weather: extremely mild, clear through the evening. A perfect day for it. Goodnight, MACU — stay safe, stay strong, and good luck.

cues.speaker: STRIDE
cues.vo: Ten thousand steps, Ron! You did it! A personal best! I'm so proud of you. I always knew you could.
